upload.title: Upload Data
upload.desc: Drag CSV file or click to select
upload.err_invalid: Please select a valid CSV file

dashboard.title: Analysis Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Independent analysis modules. Customize filters to explore reliability patterns.

jackknife.title: Maintenance Jackknife
jackknife.acute_chronic_desc: High Failure Frequency AND High Downtime. Immediate action required.
jackknife.acute_desc: Low Frequency, but High Downtime. Improve maintainability/MTTR.
jackknife.chronic_desc: High Frequency, Low Downtime. Improve reliability/MTBF.
jackknife.acceptable_desc: Low Frequency, Low Downtime. Normal operation.

criticality.title: Criticality Matrix
criticality.high_risk_desc: High Frequency AND High MTTR. Severe impact on maintainability.
criticality.high_consequence_desc: Low Frequency, but High MTTR. Difficult to repair when it fails.
criticality.high_frequency_desc: High Frequency, Low MTTR. Chronic issues, easy but annoying repairs.
criticality.low_risk_desc: Low Frequency, Low MTTR. Normal operational wear.

weibull.title: Reliability Distribution Fitting (Weibull)
weibull.desc: Fit lifetime data to statistical distributions to understand failure behavior (wear-out, random, infant mortality).

pareto.title: Pareto Analysis
pareto.desc: Identify the critical 20% causes responsible for 80% of failures.

kpi.title: KPI Trends (Monthly)
kpi.desc: Track historical maintenance performance metrics over time.
kpi.no_data: No failure logs matching current filters.

nlp.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Analysis
nlp.desc: Mine unstructured text logs for failure root causes.
nlp.mining_disabled: Comment mining is disabled by default to save resources.
nlp.click_enable: Click here to enable and run analysis.
nlp.no_comments: No comments found in dataset matching active filters.
nlp.download_warning_desc: The selected AI model is not on disk. If you press 'Download and Analyze', the system will download ~350MB. This may take several minutes depending on your connection. Once completed, it will be saved locally permanently.
nlp.phase1_desc: Downloading the Artificial Intelligence from Hugging Face to local disk. This only happens once. Please do not reload the page.
nlp.phase2_desc: Depending on the number of records (thousands), deep AI analysis can take 5 to 20 minutes without a GPU. The system is working, please do not reload the page.
nlp.bg_working: Working in background... Analyzing model {index} of {total}:
nlp.comparative_desc: This section compares the results obtained by the analyzed models. The agreement rate indicates the percentage of comments classified under the same semantic category.
nlp.not_analyzed: This model has not been analyzed yet.
nlp.click_to_download_analyze: Click here to download and analyze {tab}

kijima.title: Kijima Imperfect Repair Models (GRP)
kijima.desc: Fit Kijima I & II virtual age models (Constant and Time-Dependent) compared to traditional Weibull.
kijima.mtbf_note: The Estimated MTBF corresponds to the theoretical mean of the fitted distribution and may differ from the historical/arithmetic MTBF due to distribution skewness (e.g., when beta is far from 1).

apm.title: APM: Bad Actors & Reliability Growth
apm.desc: Asset Management Metrics and Crow-AMSAA/Nelson-Aalen Trend
apm.growth_desc: Upward curve = Accelerating wear-out. Straight line = Random.

iso_analysis.rcm_desc: Generates structured RCM decision sheets guided by Artificial Intelligence.
iso_analysis.fmeca_desc: Evaluate failure modes and quantify risk priority by calculating RPN (S x O x D).
iso_analysis.rca_desc: Implements Ishikawa (Fishbone) diagrams and 5 Whys methodology for structured failure analysis.
iso_analysis.fta_desc: Build deductive logical models with AND/OR gates to calculate top event probability from basic events.
iso_analysis.ram_desc: Simulate future plant availability and production assurance by varying logistics and preventive maintenance efficiency.
iso_analysis.copilot_desc: Interact with the Coordinator Agent to analyze failures, suggest RCM plans or diagnose problems using international standards.
iso_analysis.rcm_instruction: Select an equipment above and press "Generate AI" to start the 7-question RCM wizard.
iso_analysis.step_d_function: Define the equipment's function, including its operational context and required performance.
iso_analysis.step_q_failure: How can it fail to fulfill its function?
iso_analysis.step_d_failure: Identify ways the asset ceases to perform as desired.
iso_analysis.step_d_mode: Describe the physical event and root cause creating the loss of function.
iso_analysis.step_q_effect: What happens when the failure occurs?
iso_analysis.step_d_effect: Detail what happens when the failure mode occurs (noise, alarms, physical stop, smoke).
iso_analysis.step_d_consequence: Classify and describe safety, environmental, operational, or economic impact.
iso_analysis.step_q_task: What can be done to prevent it?
iso_analysis.step_d_task: Propose a preventive maintenance task (inspection, lubrication, cyclic replacement).
iso_analysis.step_q_alt: What to do if no preventive task is viable?
iso_analysis.step_d_alt: Define the action to take if predicting the failure is not viable (redesign, run-to-failure, procedure change).
iso_analysis.prev_efficiency_desc: Higher PM efficiency mitigates failures and significantly reduces downtime from corrective tasks.
iso_analysis.logistics_delay_desc: Logistics downtime (procurement, mobilization, tools) added to each shutdown event.
iso_analysis.production_assurance_desc: Net available capacity factor accounting for minor losses and flow restrictions.
iso_analysis.placeholder_effect: e.g., Plant shutdown due to low pressure
iso_analysis.no_modes_entered: No failure modes entered for this evaluation. Use the top panel to add one.
iso_analysis.rca_instruction: Select an equipment above and press "Analyze Failures (AI)" to generate the causal chain.
iso_analysis.fta_instruction: Design the Fault Tree by adding logical nodes hierarchically to calculate global system reliability.
iso_analysis.welcome_msg: Hello. I am your Reliability Copilot. I can assist you in applying SAE JA1011 (RCM), IEC 60812 (FMECA), IEC 62740 (RCA), and ISO 20815 (RAM) standards. How can I help you today?
iso_analysis.q1: What are the 7 steps of SAE JA1011?
iso_analysis.q2: How is RPN calculated in FMECA?
iso_analysis.q3: What does the ISO 20815 standard analyze?
iso_analysis.q4: Recommend a strategy for bearing failures
iso_analysis.res_rcm:
The SAE JA1011 standard establishes the minimum 7 sequential criteria for a process to be called RCM:
1. Functions: Asset performance in its operational context.
2. Functional failures: Total or partial loss of function.
3. Failure modes: Physical cause of the limiting event.
4. Failure effects: What happens when the mode occurs.
5. Consequences: Why it matters (safety, environment, operational).
6. Proactive tasks: Feasible preventive task and interval.
7. Alternative actions: What to do if proactive maintenance is not viable.

I recommend using our interactive RCM Wizard in its tab to automatically model these fields based on your database.
iso_analysis.res_rpn:
According to the IEC 60812 standard, the Risk Priority Number (RPN) is calculated using the formula:
RPN = Severity (S) x Occurrence (O) x Detection (D)
Where each variable is valued from 1 to 10:
- Severity: Failure impact on the overall system.
- Occurrence: Estimated frequency of the physical cause.
- Detection: Ability of control methods to identify the failure before it occurs.

You can classify criticality as: Low (<50), Medium (50-149), High (150-299), and Critical (>=300). Use our interactive FMECA Matrix to rate your equipment.
iso_analysis.res_ram:
The ISO 20815 standard defines the framework for Production Assurance based on RAM analysis:
- Reliability: Probability of failure-free operation (modeled via Kijima or Weibull).
- Availability: Proportion of uptime (Uptime / (Uptime + Downtime)).
- Maintainability: Restoration speed (MTTR).

Our RAM simulator in the Production Assurance tab models system behavior by adjusting logistics delays and PM efficiency.
iso_analysis.res_rodamiento:
For recurring bearing failures (e.g., spalling or misalignment), the suggested structured strategy is:
1. Run a Root Cause Analysis (RCA - IEC 62740) using the Ishikawa (Fishbone) diagram and 5 Whys to isolate the deep physical cause.
2. Integrate a proactive RCM task consisting of biweekly vibration spectral analysis and thermography.
3. If the RPN (FMECA) exceeds 150, evaluate installing automated volume-regulated grease injectors.
iso_analysis.res_default:
Understood. I have analyzed your query. As the Reliability Coordinator Agent, I believe this situation can be addressed through a structured analysis:
- If you need to evaluate immediate physical causes, I suggest performing an RCA / 5 Whys.
- If you seek to implement systematic predictive or preventive maintenance, use the FMECA matrix combined with RCM.

Please specify the equipment in your plant or the standard (SAE, IEC, ISO) you wish to delve into to route you to a specialist agent.
iso_analysis.copilot_welcome: Hello, I am the Reliability Coordinator. I can help you analyze plant issues, structure RCM/FMEA analysis, or interact with specialist sub-agents. How can I help you today?
